title: Kroll Continues Monthly Solana Liquidations for FTX/Alameda Creditors
summary: Kroll, managing the FTX/Alameda bankruptcy, is regularly unstaking and selling Solana (SOL) to repay creditors. This process is part of ongoing efforts to liquidate Alameda Research's assets.
body:
Kroll, acting as the bankruptcy administrator for FTX and Alameda Research, is continuing its monthly routine of unstaking and liquidating Solana (SOL) holdings. These liquidations are part of the process to repay creditors from the Alameda Research and FTX estate.

The process involves unstaking SOL and selling the assets to generate funds for creditor repayments. This has been a routine procedure since the bankruptcy proceedings began, ensuring that the estate's assets are systematically liquidated to fulfill financial obligations.

For everyday people, this means that the market may see periodic sales of SOL as part of these liquidations. While this is part of a structured repayment plan, it could potentially affect SOL's market price in the short term, depending on the volume of sales.

Investors and SOL holders should watch for any announcements regarding the scale and timing of these liquidations. Understanding the schedule and volume of these sales can help in making informed decisions about their own SOL holdings. Read more → https://x.com/martypartymusic/status/2054596837164920954
twitterThread: Kroll is regularly liquidating Solana (SOL) from the FTX/Alameda estate to repay creditors. This is part of their monthly routine.
The process involves unstaking and selling SOL holdings to generate funds. This has been ongoing since the bankruptcy proceedings began.

If you hold SOL, keep an eye on these liquidations as they could impact market prices. Read more → https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-05-13-kroll-continues-monthly-solana-liquidations-for-ftxalameda-creditors
standaloneTweet: Kroll is regularly selling Solana (SOL) from the FTX/Alameda estate to repay creditors. If you hold SOL, watch for these liquidations as they could affect market prices.
